title: Balanced Binary Tree
pattern: Tree â€” Height Check with Early Termination
coreIdea: Check if a binary tree is height-balanced (left and right subtree heights differ by at most 1 at every node) using a single bottom-up recursive pass that returns -1 as a sentinel to signal imbalance found anywhere, avoiding redundant height recomputation.
recognitionCues: asks if a tree is height-balanced at EVERY node, not just at root
naive approach recomputes height repeatedly, O(n^2); problem hints at O(n) single-pass
approach: Define a recursive function returning height, or -1 if any subtree is already found unbalanced. At each node, get left and right heights recursively; if either is -1 or their difference exceeds 1, return -1 immediately (propagating the imbalance signal upward). Otherwise return 1 + max(left, right).
